title: Transfer Lab: Repair an Emergency Shelter Intake
summary: Transfer Lab: Repair an Emergency Shelter Intake retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar shelter intake hierarchy and feedback incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply design user goals, design information hierarchy, design spacing alignment, design color typography, design consistency feedback, design prototype test in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar shelter intake hierarchy and feedback incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Unfamiliar shelter intake hierarchy and feedback incident case 1: treat every requested feature as equally urgent so no stakeholder preference is challenged.

Unfamiliar shelter intake hierarchy and feedback incident case 1: separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Unfamiliar shelter intake hierarchy and feedback incident case 1: choose colors and components first because visual polish reveals the correct user task.
